summary:
Given n nodes labeled 0 to n - 1 and a list of undirected edges, count how many connected components the graph has.

A connected component is a maximal set of nodes where any node can be reached from any other through the edges. A node with no edges forms its own component.

examples.explanation: Nodes 0, 1, and 2 form one component and nodes 3, 4 form another, giving 2 components.
All five nodes are linked into a single chain, so there is just 1 component.

constraints: Each edge connects two distinct nodes in the range 0 to n - 1
Isolated nodes each count as their own component
